req.title: Procedure record (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-061 mandates that a clinician can record a Procedure tied to an open Encounter, with strict validation on mandatory fields and encounter state. The suite covers: (1) EP positive – a fully valid payload returns 201 with stored procedure; (2) NEG – missing encounter reference yields 422; (3) NEG – permission-denied (non-clinician role) yields 403; (4) STC – posting a procedure against a closed/discharged encounter triggers a state-transition conflict (422/409); (5) BVA – procedure code field at exactly 1 character (minimum boundary) to probe field-length validation. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own Encounter resources.

tests.name: test_create_procedure_valid_encounter_returns_201
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
An open Encounter exists via <open_encounter_id> fixture
actions: Build a Procedure payload with resourceType, status=completed, code, and subject/encounter references
POST payload to /Procedure with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_auth_token>
expected: Response body encounter.reference matches the open encounter id
trace: R-HLT-061 – happy path: procedure stored when encounter is open

tests.name: test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
actions: Build a Procedure payload that omits the encounter reference entirely
POST payload to /Procedure with valid clinician token
expected: Response body contains an error detail referencing encounter as missing mandatory element
trace: R-HLT-061 – error pathway: 422 when encounter missing

tests.name: test_create_procedure_non_clinician_role_returns_403
preconditions: A non-clinician (e.g. read-only patient portal) auth token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
An open Encounter exists via <open_encounter_id> fixture
actions: Build a fully valid Procedure payload referencing the open encounter
POST payload to /Procedure using the non-clinician token

tests.name: test_create_procedure_closed_encounter_returns_conflict
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A discharged/closed Encounter exists via <closed_encounter_id> fixture
actions: Build a Procedure payload referencing the closed/discharged Encounter
POST payload to /Procedure with clinician token
expected: Response body indicates invalid state transition or encounter is not in an active state
trace: R-HLT-061 – conflicting state transition: procedure against a closed encounter

tests.name: test_create_procedure_code_at_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
An open Encounter exists via <open_encounter_id> fixture
API documents minimum procedure code length as 1 character
actions: Build a Procedure payload where the code.coding[0].code is exactly 1 character (minimum boundary value 'X')
POST payload to /Procedure with clinician token
expected: HTTP 201 Created (1-char code is at valid minimum boundary)
Response body contains id confirming resource was stored
trace: R-HLT-061 – BVA on procedure code field minimum length boundary (1 char)

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```json
{
  "requirement_id": "R-HLT-061",
  "reasoning": "R-HLT-061 mandates that a clinician can record a Procedure tied to an open Encounter, with strict validation on mandatory fields and encounter state. The suite covers: (1) EP positive – a fully valid payload returns 201 with stored procedure; (2) NEG – missing encounter reference yields 422; (3) NEG – permission-denied (non-clinician role) yields 403; (4) STC – posting a procedure against a closed/discharged encounter triggers a state-transition conflict (422/409); (5) BVA – procedure code field at exactly 1 character (minimum boundary) to probe field-length validation. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own Encounter resources.",
  "tests": [
    {
      "name": "test_create_procedure_valid_encounter_returns_201",
      "kind": "positive",
      "heuristic": "EP",
      "preconditions": [
        "FHIR-lite service is reachable",
        "Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture",
        "An open Encounter exists via <open_encounter_id> fixture"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Build a Procedure payload with resourceType, status=completed, code, and subject/encounter references",
        "POST payload to /Procedure with Authorization: Bearer <clinician_auth_token>"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 201 Created",
        "Response body contains id field",
        "Response body encounter.reference matches the open encounter id"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-HLT-061 – happy path: procedure stored when encounter is open"
      ],
      "executable": "import pytest\nimport requests\n\ndef test_create_procedure_valid_encounter_returns_201(\n    base_url, clinician_auth_token, open_encounter_id\n):\n    payload = {\n        \"resourceType\": \"Procedure\",\n        \"status\": \"completed\",\n        \"code\": {\n            \"coding\": [{\"system\": \"http://snomed.info/sct\", \"code\": \"80146002\"}]\n        },\n        \"subject\": {\"reference\": \"Patient/test-patient-1\"},\n        \"encounter\": {\"reference\": f\"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}\"}\n    }\n    resp = requests.post(\n        f\"{base_url}/Procedure\",\n        json=payload,\n        headers={\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}\"}\n    )\n    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text\n    body = resp.json()\n    assert \"id\" in body\n    assert body[\"encounter\"][\"reference\"] == f\"Encounter/{open_encounter_id}\"\n"
    },
    {
      "name": "test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422",
      "kind": "negative",
      "heuristic": "NEG",
      "preconditions": [
        "FHIR-lite service is reachable",
        "Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture"
      ],
      "actions": [
        "Build a Procedure payload that omits the encounter reference entirely",
        "POST payload to /Procedure with valid clinician token"
      ],
      "expected": [
        "HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity",
        "Response body contains an error detail referencing encounter as missing mandatory element"
      ],
      "trace": [
        "R-HLT-061 – error pathway: 422 when encounter missing"
      ],
      "executable": "import requests\n\ndef test_create_procedure_missing_encounter_returns_422(\n    base_url, clinician_auth_token\n):\n    payload = {\n        \"resourceType\": \"Procedure\",\n        \"status\": \"completed\",\n        \"code\": {\n            \"coding\": [{\"system\": \"http://snomed.info/sct\", \"code\": \"80146002\"}]\n        },\n        \"subject\": {\"reference\": \"Patient/test-patient-1\"}\n        # encounter intentionally omitted\n    }\n    resp = requests.post(\n        f\"{base_url}/Procedure\",\n        json=payload,\n        headers={\"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}\"}\n    )\n    assert resp.status_code == 422, resp.text\n    body = resp.json()\n    error_text = str(body).lower()\n    assert \"encounter\" in error_text\n"
